name: Asymmetry Fine Sweep
carving_goal: Distinguish sharp critical point vs smooth crossover vs non-monotone vs null for basin selection transition.

categorization.basin_position_behavior: Both LOW and HIGH basin E values increase monotonically with asymmetry. LOW E goes from 73.6 (asym=-0.30) to 77.4 (asym=+0.30). HIGH E goes from 87.3 to 90.5. This is a SMOOTH, continuous deformation of the energy landscape.
categorization.basin_selection_behavior: HIGH rate shows no systematic trend in the [-0.3, +0.3] range. Values bounce between 0% and 60% with no monotone pattern. No sharp critical point. No smooth crossover. The selection is dominated by per-episode RNG initialization, not by asymmetry within this range.

combined_data_with_phase_H.asym_neg01: Phase H: 4/5 (80%) vs Phase K: 2/5 (40%). Combined: 6/10 (60%). High variance between sessions.
combined_data_with_phase_H.asym_pos01: Phase H: 1/5 (20%) vs Phase K: 1/5 (20%). Combined: 2/10 (20%). Consistent.
combined_data_with_phase_H.note: The 'sign-flip' from Phase H (negative asym → more HIGH) partially replicates but is weaker than initial 80%/20% split suggested.

at_large_asym_from_phase_H.asym_pos1: 1/5 HIGH, max E=94.57 (elevated, one splat event)

pre_registration_evaluation.sharp_critical_point: KILLED — no asym value shows HIGH rate jumping from ≥50% to ≤10% within 0.05 width
pre_registration_evaluation.smooth_crossover: KILLED (for selection) — HIGH rate is not monotonically decreasing
pre_registration_evaluation.non_monotone: MATCHES (for selection) — HIGH rate oscillates, but this is noise not coupling
pre_registration_evaluation.null: PARTIAL (for selection) — systematic trend is absent in [-0.3, +0.3]

no_systematic_trend_weakens_order_param.note: The basin SELECTION has no trend in [-0.3, +0.3]. But basin POSITION shifts monotonically. Asymmetry is an order parameter for WHERE the basins sit, but NOT for WHICH basin the system falls into (within this range).

key_finding: Asymmetry decomposes into two distinct effects: (1) a SMOOTH, monotone shift of basin energy positions (confirmed), and (2) basin SELECTION probability which is noise-dominated in [-0.3, +0.3] and only becomes deterministic at |asym| ≥ 0.5 where bistability collapses.
